entries.quote: We are excited to introduce the RuPay variant to the existing co-branded AU Small Finance Bank travel credit card. With seamless UPI integration and comprehensive insurance coverage, this card is designed to meet the evolving needs of modern travellers, especially those from tier 2, 3 & 4 cities.
entries.signal: ixigo is expanding co-branded fintech reach via RuPay+UPI integration strategy aimed at underserved Bharat markets—positioning travel credit cards as UPI-enabled products to deepen payment-on-card adoption.
entries.belief: [Inferred · High] ixigo views co-branded cards paired with UPI credit infrastructure as a strategic lever to reach tier 2–4 users and differentiate against competitors; prioritizing accessibility & financial inclusion over metro-centric premium positioning.

entries.quote: Brevistay has built a category-defining platform with deep hotel partnerships and a strong presence in the flexible-stay segment. We see significant opportunities to leverage technology, AI, and distribution to create a more comprehensive accommodation ecosystem for Indian travellers while helping our hotel partners maximise utilisation and revenue.
entries.signal: ixigo is aggressively closing its full-stack OTA gap — hotels are the final piece; combining Brevistay's 10,000+ directly contracted hotels (incl. Tier II/III) with ixigo's AI layer signals a direct move to own the end-to-end trip journey, reducing reliance on aggregator partners and narrowing cross-sell white space for travel fintech players like Scapia.
entries.belief: [Inferred · High] Management believes that owning hotel supply + AI-native trip planning will lock in the Bharat traveller within the ixigo ecosystem, making ancillary financial products (travel credit cards, BNPL) harder for third-party fintechs to wedge in.

entries.quote: It's not a zero-sum game. The real question isn't where users book—it's whether they book online at all.
entries.signal: ixigo shifting focus from market-share competition to expanding *category adoption*, particularly in hotels (20% online penetration), suggesting long-tail monetization over incumbent battles.
entries.belief: [Inferred · High] Management believes trust-building and behavioural change—not discounting—is the sustainable path; hotels represent massive Bharat offline-to-online conversion opportunity.
